title: 10 best herbal tinctures for hematuria
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for hematuria are concentrated liquid extracts made from plants, herbs, or botanicals that have been traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms of blood in the urine. These tinctures work by addressing the underlying causes of hematuria, such as inflammation, irritation, and infection, and promoting healing and repair in the urinary tract. For example, goldenrod and corn silk tinctures have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce bleeding and soothe the mucous membranes in the bladder. Regular use of these herbal tinctures can improve lives by reducing the frequency and severity of hematuria episodes, allowing individuals to better manage their symptoms and maintain a sense of normalcy and comfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bearberry tinctures helps with hematuria because it contains a unique compound called arbutin, which is converted to hydroquinone in the body. Hydroquinone has been shown to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing the frequency and severity of urinary tract infections that can cause hematuria. Additionally, bearberry's natural astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels and reduce inflammation, which can also contribute to its ability to alleviate hematuria symptoms. By targeting these underlying causes, bearberry tinctures can provide relief from hematuria and promote overall urinary health.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Bearberry tincture's astringent properties help to reduce inflammation and shrink the size of blood vessels, which can help to alleviate bleeding in the urinary tract.

Antioxidant activity: Bearberry tincture's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the kidneys and bladder tissues, contributing to hematuria.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Bearberry tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as arbutin and uva-ursi acid, help to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can alleviate symptoms of hematuria, including pain and burning during urination.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and improve urinary tract health.
Barks: High in astringent properties that can help to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding associated with hematuria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh arctostaphylos uva ursi leaves and stems on a dry sunny day.
Cut the harvested material into small pieces and pack it tightly into a clean glass jar.
Pour 200ml of high-proof vodka over the packed herb material to achieve a 1:2 ratio.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the mixture daily, allowing the tincture to macerate.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and labeling the final product.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with hematuria because it has been traditionally used to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the urinary tract. The herb's demulcent properties create a protective barrier that helps to reduce inflammation and irritation, thereby reducing the bleeding associated with hematuria. Additionally, marshmallow's anti-inflammatory properties help to alleviate discomfort and pain, providing relief for those experiencing this condition. By targeting the root cause of the issue, herbal marshmallow tinctures can promote natural healing and recovery from hematuria.
remedy_properties: Demulcent properties: Marshmallow root's mucilages coat and soothe the urinary tract lining, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by hematuria, thereby promoting healing and comfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow root contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which help to reduce swelling and pain in the urinary tract, alleviating symptoms of hematuria.

Antioxidant properties: Marshmallow root's antioxidants help protect the urinary tract from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of chronic inflammation and promoting overall kidney health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high mucilage content, which helps soothe and protect the urinary tract lining, alleviating symptoms of hematuria.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes contain a high concentration of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract and alleviate bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marshmallow roots and cut into small pieces.
Combine the chopped roots with vodka at a ratio of 1:2 for maceration.
Steep the mixture in a dark glass container for 14 days, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth, discarding solids.
Transfer the resulting tincture to amber glass bottles and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal tinctures helps with hematuria because its active compound, berberine, has potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Berberine binds to the mucous membranes in the urinary tract, reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial adhesion, which can contribute to the condition. Additionally, berberine's antibacterial effects help combat underlying infections that may be causing hematuria. By addressing these underlying causes, goldenseal tinctures can provide relief from painful and frequent urination associated with hematuria, promoting a healthier and more comfortable urinary system.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine, a key alkaloid in Goldenseal, has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections in the urinary tract, which can contribute to hematuria.

Berbamine: Berbamine, another important alkaloid in Goldenseal, exhibits antioxidant and free-radical-scavenging activities that can help protect the urinary tract from oxidative damage and prevent further irritation of the bladder lining, leading to reduced bleeding.

Saponins: Saponins, a class of compounds present in Goldenseal, have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help calm irritated mucous membranes in the bladder and urethra, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with hematuria.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in berberine, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it effective in reducing inflammation and treating urinary tract infections associated with hematuria.
Rhyzomes: Containing high amounts of alkaloids like berberine, rhyzomes help to combat bacterial and fungal infections, alleviating symptoms of hematuria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh or dried goldenseal root hydrastis canadensis in late summer or early fall.
Clean and chop the goldenseal roots into small pieces using a sharp knife and sterilized cutting board.
Combine the chopped goldenseal roots with 100 proof vodka in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking daily for 30 seconds.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with hematuria because it has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the urinary tract, reducing inflammation and irritation. The tannins present in licorice also help to shrink hemorrhoids and reduce bleeding by constricting blood vessels. Additionally, the saponins in licorice have antispasmodic effects, relaxing muscle contractions in the urinary tract and allowing for a smoother flow of urine. This combination of actions helps to alleviate hematuria symptoms, promoting a faster recovery and reducing discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice root tincture helps reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can alleviate symptoms of hematuria such as pain and discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in licorice root tincture help protect the kidneys from oxidative damage, reducing the risk of kidney damage and promoting overall urinary health.
Hormone-balancing properties: Licorice root tincture helps regulate hormonal imbalances that can contribute to hematuria, such as fluctuations in estrogen levels.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant have been traditionally used to treat urinary tract infections and inflammation, making them effective in treating hematuria.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain flavonoids and glycyrrhizin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe and heal irritated bladder tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra in the morning after dew has evaporated and before midday.
Cleanse the harvested roots by rinsing them thoroughly under running water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces using a sharp knife or food processor to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine one part of chopped glycyrrhiza glabra root with two parts of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar and steep for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place.

Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar and discard the solids, then store the tincture in the refrigerator for up to 6 months at a concentration of 1:5.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Corn tinctures helps with hematuria because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the urinary tract and reduce inflammation. The tannins in corn help to constrict blood vessels, decreasing bleeding and reducing the risk of further irritation. Additionally, corn's natural astringent properties help to shrink swollen tissue, promoting healing and reducing discomfort associated with hematuria. As a result, herbal corn tinctures can provide relief from frequent urination, pain, and bloody urine, helping to alleviate symptoms and promote overall urinary health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal corn tinctures containing plants like Epilobium angustifolium (willowherb) or Hypericum perforatum (St. John's Wort) have anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to hematuria.

Astringent property: Herbs like Uva ursi (bearberry), Cornus officinalis (corn tincture), and Plantago major (plantain) contain astringent compounds that help constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding in the urinary tract, thus alleviating hematuria.

Antioxidant property: Herbal corn tinctures rich in antioxidants, such as those containing Quercetin or Anthocyanins, can help protect the urinary tract from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to hematuria.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the corn plant contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract and alleviate symptoms of hematuria.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, which are underground stems, have been used in traditional medicine to treat kidney stones and other urinary tract issues, making them a valuable part for creating tinctures for hematuria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zea mays leaves and stems on a sunny day when the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested material in a warm oven or using a dehydrator for 12 hours.
Chop 200g of dried zea mays into small pieces and combine with 400ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a dark cupboard for 4 weeks, shaking it daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper tinctures helps with hematuria because they possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herbal extract has been found to effectively reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which is often a underlying cause of hematuria. Additionally, bell peppers are rich in vitamin C, which has been shown to help reduce oxidative stress and promote overall kidney health. By addressing these root causes, bell pepper tinctures can provide natural relief from hematuria symptoms, promoting a healthier and more balanced urinary tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: Bell pepper roots contain a high concentration of flavonoids and saponins, which help reduce inflammation and promote urinary tract health.
Barks: The barks of the bell pepper plant contain tannins, which act as natural astringents to help reduce bleeding and soothe irritated bladder lining.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh capsicum annuum leaves and flowers on a sunny day in the morning.
Clean the harvested plant material by gently rinsing it with filtered water and blotting dry.
Combine one part of the cleaned plant material with five parts of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for six weeks, shaking the jar twice daily to extract the bioactive compounds.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into dark glass bottles, discarding the solids and storing the liquid at 2-8°c.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Bell peppers contain antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene, which could help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, potentially alleviating some symptoms associated with hematuria.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Bell peppers also contain anti-inflammatory compounds like capsaicinoids, which might help reduce inflammation and swelling in the urinary tract and kidneys, potentially reducing symptoms of hematuria.
Antimicrobial properties: The antioxidant and antimicrobial compounds present in bell peppers could potentially help prevent bacterial or fungal infections that may contribute to hematuria.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with hematuria because of its natural diuretic properties, which can help to flush out the urinary tract and reduce inflammation. The plant's bitter compounds also stimulate digestion and absorption, promoting a healthy gut and reducing bloating and discomfort associated with hematuria. Additionally, dandelion's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects can soothe and protect the urinary tract, reducing bleeding and alleviating symptoms of this condition. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion tincture contains flavonoids and terpenes that reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with hematuria.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in dandelion tincture help protect the kidneys and bladder from oxidative stress, which can contribute to hematuria by reducing the damage caused by free radicals.
Diuretic properties: Dandelion tincture stimulates urine production and helps eliminate excess water and toxins from the body, which can help reduce the frequency and intensity of hematuria episodes.

Seeds: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol, which reduces inflammation in the kidneys and bladder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of fresh or dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers on a sunny day.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and combine it with 80% vodka in a large glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place allowing the plant compounds to infuse into the solvent.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Store the filtered tincture in a dark glass bottle and use it within 6 months after preparation or for up to 2 years in the fridge.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto tinctures helps with hematuria because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and irritation in the urinary tract. The herb's ability to block the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT) also contributes to its benefits, as DHT can exacerbate symptoms of benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH), a common cause of hematuria. Additionally, saw palmetto has been shown to improve urine flow and reduce prostate size, further alleviating pressure on the urinary tract and reducing the occurrence of blood in the urine.
remedy_properties: 5-alpha-reductase inhibition: Saw palmetto tincture helps reduce the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), which can contribute to enlarged prostate and benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH), common causes of hematuria.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Saw palmetto's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, alleviating symptoms such as burning or stinging sensations during urination that are associated with hematuria.

Pro-apoptotic properties: Some studies suggest that saw palmetto may promote the death of cancer cells, which can contribute to the development of hematuria; this property may also help reduce the risk of tumor growth and recurrence.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in fatty acids and phytosterols, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of hematuria.
Seeds: Saw palmetto seeds contain a unique compound called beta-sitosterol, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe the urinary tract and relieve hematuria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh serenoa repens leaves and stems in the spring or fall, ensuring optimal potency.
Chop 250 grams of harvested material into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine chopped plant material with 750 milliliters of pure vodka in a glass jar, leaving one inch headspace.
Store the mixture at room temperature for six weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate solvent diffusion.
Strain and filter the liquid through cheesecloth or coffee filters into dark glass bottles, discarding solids and storing the tincture in a cool, dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Juniper tinctures helps with hematuria because its active compounds, specifically terpinene-4-ol and bornyl acetate, have been shown to reduce inflammation and relieve spasms in the urinary tract. The antiseptic properties of juniper also help to eliminate bacteria that can cause or exacerbate hematuria. Additionally, juniper's diuretic effects help to increase urine flow, which can help to flush out red blood cells and other debris that may be contributing to the condition. remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Juniper tincture's antispasmodic property helps to relax and soothe the muscles in the urinary tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can contribute to hematuria.
Anti-inflammatory property: Juniper tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the kidneys and ureters, which can alleviate pain and discomfort associated with hematuria.
Diuretic property: Juniper tincture's diuretic properties help to increase urine production and flow, which can help to flush out toxins and excess fluids that may be contributing to hematuria.
remedy_parts: Roots: Juniper roots are rich in terpenoids and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and pain associated with hematuria.
Bark: The juniper bark is rich in lignans, which have antiseptic and astringent properties, helping to reduce bleeding and soothe irritated tissues in the urinary tract.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of dried juniperus communis berries and transfer them to a clean glass jar.
Add 200 milliliters of vodka at 70% abv to the jar, ensuring the herbs are completely covered.
Securely seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 14 days, allowing the mixture to steep.
After 14 days, strain the liquid through a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Label and store the tincture in a cool dark place for up to two years, using 20-30 drops per dose three times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Silver birch tinctures helps with hematuria because its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the urinary tract, alleviating symptoms such as blood in the urine. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of betula pendula, collecting approximately 500g for the tincture.
Dry the leaves using air circulation or low heat until they reach a brittle consistency.
Combine 250g of dried betula pendula leaves with 750ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool, dark place for four to six weeks, shaking daily.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry tinctures helps with hematuria because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with hematuria, including pain, frequency, and blood in the urine. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried vaccinium macrocarpon berries in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Cleanse and chop one part vaccinium macrocarpon berries with two parts 80% ethanol spirits for five minutes.
Combine the chopped berry mixture with the ethanol spirits in a clean glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for four weeks.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and preserving the liquid.
Label and date the final tincture product, using 30 drops twice daily as directed to alleviate hematuria symptoms within two hours of consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zucchini tinctures helps with hematuria because of its unique combination of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and bioactive molecules. The tincture's ability to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract helps alleviate symptoms associated with hematuria, such as pain and discomfort. Additionally, the antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and damage to the bladder lining, promoting a healthy environment for urination and reducing the occurrence of blood in urine. Diuretic and anti-spasmodic properties of herbs like Juniper Berry (Juniperus communis) and Uva Ursi (Arctostaphylos uva-ursi): These properties may help increase urine production, reduce discomfort associated with frequent urination, and relax the urinary tract muscles.

Astringent and anti-inflammatory properties of herbs like Marshmallow Root (Althaea officinalis) and Goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis): These properties may help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the urinary tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with hematuria.

remedy_recipe: Harvest cucurbita pepo roots in late fall or early spring when they are dry and free of leaves.
Chop the roots into small pieces to release their medicinal properties and then combine them with 70% vodka at a ratio of 10:1.
Transfer the mixture to a clean glass jar and store it in a dark place for a minimum of 4 weeks to allow maceration.
Shake the jar daily during the first week to enhance extraction and then strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter after 4 weeks.
Bottle the filtered tincture and store it in the refrigerator to slow down oxidation and preserve its potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tinctures helps with hematuria because it contains bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce bleeding and alleviate symptoms. The tannins present in nettle leaves bind to the blood vessels, constricting them and reducing blood flow to the affected area. Additionally, the anticoagulant properties of nettle may help to prevent clotting and promote healthy circulation, thereby minimizing further bleeding. Antioxidant properties: Nettle tincture is rich in antioxidants, which help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, thereby reducing the risk of kidney damage and promoting overall renal health.

Uricosuric activity: Stinging nettle has been shown to have uricosuric effects, helping to increase the excretion of uric acid in the urine, which can help alleviate symptoms of hematuria caused by excess uric acid.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried urtica dioica leaves and stems in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Cleanly chop one part of plant material into small pieces using scissors or pruning shears.
Combine chopped plant material with 70% vodka or high-proof grain alcohol at a ratio of 1:4 for three weeks.
Shake the mixture vigorously daily during a six-week maceration period in a dark glass container.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean dark glass bottle and store at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover tinctures helps with hematuria because it contains isoflavones, particularly biochanin A and formononetin, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract and promote healing, thereby alleviating symptoms of hematuria such as blood in the urine. Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in red clover tincture help protect the kidneys from oxidative stress and damage, reducing the risk of further bleeding or kidney damage that can occur with hematuria.

Vasoprotective compounds: Red clover tincture contains vasoprotective compounds such as formononetin and biochanin A, which have been shown to improve blood vessel tone and reduce capillary fragility, helping to prevent further bleeding or leakage that can contribute to hematuria.

remedy_recipe: Harvest trifolium pratense flowers at peak blooming in sunny fields or meadows, selecting only fresh and fragrant blooms for quality tincture.
Combine one part of the freshly harvested trifolium pratense flowers with two parts of 95% grain-based vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring a consistent ratio for optimal extraction.
Store the jar in a dark cabinet or pantry, allowing the mixture to steep and extract for at least six weeks, shaking gently every few days to enhance solvent action.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, squeezing out any remaining liquid from the herb material before discarding the solids.

Fill dark-tinted amber glass bottles with the filtered trifolium pratense tincture and label them with the date, ingredients, and recommended dosages for safe storage and future use at 30-60 drops three times daily.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves in the early morning or late evening when dew is present.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: The antioxidant compounds present in Ginkgo biloba tincture, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to hematuria by protecting the kidneys from damage.

Anti-inflammatory Effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Maidenhair Tree tincture, including ginkgolides and bilobalide, may help to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can alleviate symptoms of hematuria such as pain and discomfort during urination.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried ginkgo biloba leaves in quantities of 50 grams and store them in an airtight container until use.
Grind the ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for approximately 30 seconds to release essential oils.
Mix 1 part ground ginkgo biloba with 4 parts high-proof vodka in a glass jar, shaking gently for 5 minutes to ensure uniform extraction.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking it daily, allowing the tincture to steep and extract beneficial compounds from the ginkgo biloba leaves.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another glass container, discarding the solids, and label the final product for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle tinctures helps with hematuria because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the bladder lining, reducing inflammation and irritation. The silymarin compounds in milk thistle also possess antioxidant capabilities, which help protect the urinary tract from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals. As a result, milk thistle tinctures can help alleviate hematuria symptoms such as blood in the urine, pain, and discomfort, promoting overall urinary health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Milk thistle tincture helps protect the kidneys from damage caused by oxidative stress, which can contribute to hematuria (blood in urine).

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and silymarin present in milk thistle tincture reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of hematuria such as blood in urine.
Hepatoprotective properties: Milk thistle tincture supports liver function, which is crucial for proper kidney function and can help prevent or alleviate hematuria caused by liver-related conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silymarin, a potent flavonoid complex that helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with hematuria.
Seeds: Contain a high concentration of flavonoids and terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that aid in treating hematuria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry silybum marianum flowers in a warm place for at least seven days.
Grind dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix one part powdered silybum marianum with two parts vodka or high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks to allow for maceration and extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another container discarding solids then store tincture in dark glass bottles at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with hematuria because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the urinary tract. The curcuminoids present in turmeric have been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory cytokines, thus decreasing the likelihood of blood clot formation and subsequent bleeding into the urine. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage kidney tissues, further contributing to its therapeutic benefits in alleviating hematuria symptoms.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Turmeric's active compound curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the urinary tract, potentially reducing the occurrence and severity of hematuria.

Bioavailability Enhancers: Turmeric tinctures contain bioavailability enhancers like piperine, which can increase the absorption and utilization of curcuminoids by the body, allowing them to reach the bloodstream and target areas more effectively in reducing hematuria symptoms.

Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: Turmeric tinctures also contain other anti-inflammatory compounds like turmerin, which has been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory effects on the urinary tract, potentially helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with hematuria such as pain and frequency.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes are rich in curcumin, a potent bioactive compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with hematuria.

Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant contain a wealth of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in treating hematuria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh turmeric roots and clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Cut the cleaned turmeric roots into small pieces and dry them in shade for 7-10 days.
Combine the dried turmeric root pieces with 80-proof vodka or other high-proof spirits at a ratio of 1:5.
Store the mixture in a dark glass container for 14-21 days, shaking it daily to ensure thorough extraction.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter and store the tincture in dark glass bottles at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with hematuria because they contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can cause blood to appear in the urine. Additionally, elder tincture's ability to stimulate diuresis can help to flush out excess substances from the body, further reducing the occurrence of hematuria.
remedy_properties: Astringent and anti-inflammatory properties: Elder tincture helps to reduce bleeding by constricting blood vessels and reducing inflammation, which in turn reduces the amount of blood present in the urine.

Antioxidant and antimicrobial properties: Elder tincture's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage kidney tissues, while its antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial infections that may contribute to hematuria.

Immune-modulating and anti-inflammatory flavonoids: The flavonoids present in elder tincture have been shown to modulate the immune response, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can help to alleviate symptoms of hematuria such as burning or stinging sensations.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots have been traditionally used to treat urinary issues, including hematuria, due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.

Rhyzomes: The rhizome of the Elder plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that may help alleviate symptoms of hematuria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality sambucus nigra leaves and flowers in late summer or early fall.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material to prevent contamination and spoilage.
Chop the dried sambucus nigra into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine 250 grams of chopped sambucus nigra with 1000 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for six weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat hematuria with herbal tinctures is by combining Goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis) and Uva Ursi (Arctostaphylos uva-ursi) in equal parts. Take 20-30 drops, three times a day, for up to two weeks. This combination helps to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections that can cause bleeding. Consult with an herbalist or healthcare professional before starting treatment, as individual results may vary.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hematuria that are treated with herbal tinctures are urinary tract infections (UTIs), cystitis, and prostatitis. Herbal tinctures such as Uva Ursi, Marshmallow, and Goldenseal have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help alleviate symptoms like burning during urination, frequency, and urgency. They also support the body's natural defense against infection and promote overall urinary tract health.
